---
title: "lme_predict"
generated: true
description: "Per-element LME prediction on a (new) design `X`."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.stats.lme`](../nitrix-stats-lme.qmd)

*Re-exported here; defined in `nitrix.stats.lme._blup`.*

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:lme_predict -->
```python
lme_predict(result: Any, X: Float[Array, 'N p'], *, z: Optional[Float[Array, 'N r']] = None, group: Optional[Int[Array, ' N']] = None, level: PredictLevel = 'population') -> Float[Array, 'V N']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:lme_predict -->
Per-element LME prediction on a (new) design `X`.

`level='population'` (default) returns the marginal mean
$X\hat{\beta}$ (defined for **every** tier).  `level='conditional'`
adds the subject-specific random-effect contribution
$Z b_{\mathrm{group}}$ using the fitted modes ([`ranef`](../nitrix-stats-lme/ranef.qmd)), so the
result must come from [`lme_fit`](../nitrix-stats-lme-reml/lme_fit.qmd) under `retain_blups=True`.  A
`group` label outside the fitted range (an unseen subject) -- or
`group=None` -- falls back to the marginal mean.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:lme_predict -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `result` | `Any` | — | — | An LME result ([`REMLResult`](../nitrix-stats.qmd), [`LMEResult`](../nitrix-stats.qmd), or another fitted-LME result carrying `beta_hat` and, for the conditional level, `blups`). |
| `X` | `Float` | `N` × `p` | — | `(N, p)` fixed-effect design (same columns as the fit). |
| `z` | `Optional[Float[Array, 'N r']]` | — | `None` | `(N, r)` random-effect design for the new rows (a random-slope fit); `None` for a scalar intercept. |
| `group` | `Optional[Int[Array, ' N']]` | — | `None` | `(N,)` integer group labels indexing the fitted levels `0..q-1`; out-of-range / `None` -> the marginal mean for those rows. |
| `level` | `PredictLevel` | — | `'population'` | `'population'` (marginal) or `'conditional'` (subject-specific BLUP). |

**Returns** `Float` — shape `V` × `N`. The `(V, N)` predicted means, one length-`N` vector per element. Differentiable with respect to `X` / `z` (and the fitted coefficients / modes).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:lme_predict -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `N` | observations — time points, subjects, or sampled points |
| `p` | fixed-effect coefficients / design-matrix columns |
| `V` | elements of a mass-univariate map — voxels, vertices, or fixels |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
